name: Python
description: The dominant programming language for AI and machine learning. Python's simplicity, vast ecosystem of scientific computing libraries, and strong community make it the lingua franca of AI engineering.
whyUseIt: Every major AI framework, model, and tool is built for Python first. NumPy, Pandas, PyTorch, TensorFlow, scikit-learn, Hugging Face — all Python. You cannot be an AI engineer without Python fluency.
aiCapabilities: Python is the foundation language for AI development. Its ecosystem includes PyTorch, TensorFlow, scikit-learn, Hugging Face Transformers, and virtually every AI library.
engineerWorkflow: An AI engineer uses Python for everything: data preparation with Pandas, model training with PyTorch, API development with FastAPI, scripting with standard library, and notebook-based exploration with Jupyter.
managerExpects: Proficiency is assumed. A manager expects clean, type-hinted, well-tested Python code. They expect you to use Python idiomatically — list comprehensions, context managers, dataclasses — and to choose the right library for each task.
aiWorkflowImpact: Python's GIL can be a bottleneck for CPU-bound work, but GPU operations (PyTorch, TensorFlow) and async I/O (API calls) bypass this. For high-performance inference, Python often wraps optimised C/Rust code.
demoDescription: Install Python 3.11+, create a virtual environment, and run: `import numpy as np; import pandas as pd; print(np.random.randn(3,3)); print(pd.DataFrame({'col': [1,2,3]}).describe())`. You have just used the two most important AI libraries.

vocabulary.definition: An isolated Python installation that keeps project dependencies separate. Created with `python -m venv .venv`.
Python's package installer. `pip install numpy` downloads and installs the NumPy library.
Optional annotations that specify variable and function types: `def train(data: pd.DataFrame) -> float`. Helps with code quality and IDE support.

beginnerMistakes: Not using virtual environments and polluting the system Python installation
Writing loops when vectorised NumPy/Pandas operations exist
Ignoring type hints in AI codebases — they catch bugs early and improve readability
Not pinning dependency versions in requirements.txt or pyproject.toml
